rationale: 'An open formula is a wff with at least one free variable' rests on the free-variable notion def:fv, which was missing -- exactly as at the parallel nodes 0018 and 0020. def:wff-pred (present) is the substrate.
notes: IMPORTANT duplication: this node (id def:predicate) is titled 'Open Formula' and so is def:open-formula-quantifiers (0018) -- two same-named nodes, BOTH of which were missing def:fv. Strong candidate to deduplicate. Also def:predicate-symbol (Predicate Symbol -- 'syntactic atomic-formula-forming vocabulary') is a questionable dep: an open formula is a wff with a free variable, not intrinsically about predicate symbols; kept pending review (likely an artifact of the 'def:predicate' id) rather than removed on uncertain grounds.
